title: Caulinia retusa
taxonomy: Caulinia retusa is classified as a type of hydrophyte, specifically a member of the family Haloragaceae. It is a genus of aquatic plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Caulinia retusa belongs to the order Haloragales, which comprises various families of flowering plants. This plant is also known to be part of the class Magnoliopsida, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants. Caulinia retusa's classification is further supported by its placement within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Caulinia retusa, also known as Cauline, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
